author: yorickdowne
content:
Yes we’d love a community call. I’d also love to run a block timing diagram past you (later, need to be at PC) because I’m still trying to understand the nuances.

When is good for you? Do you have a few dates you want to put out?

I just had a call with Jason yesterday and his take is that commit boost will very much be supported by Prysm through the proxy field, and that’s he’s been noodling on this with Terence.

Differing messages from different people - getting clarity will be hugely beneficial.

author: potuz
content:
There are different components of what these middleware do: blind signing of a block without knowing one of the components and multiplexing to a bunch of players the request for a block. 

Blind signing Prysm will do over my firing. Multiplexing will be absorbed by us, so no need to do it anymore. I assume what Jason means is that contacting a preconf gateway or a relay will be covered by the usual builder API and supported by the fact that we have different URL and DATA signed in the messages.

author: potuz
content: As for dates, lets' schedule something over DMs when you have some tentative dates

author: jvranek
content:
> I assume what Jason means is that contacting a preconf gateway or a relay will be covered by the usual builder API and supported by the fact that we have different URL and DATA signed in the messages.

yes this

> blind signing of a block without knowing one of the components

and not this - we never want to touch validator keys unless explicitly needed for commitments, and in this case it's over a different signing domain by construction

separating URL and DATA is essential bc it removes the issue of: either accept no auths and risk builders not servicing you or sign the auths yourself which changes security assumptions

author: yorickdowne
content: Sent you a fren request for scheduling
